specification.topic-statement: The JSON allocator bridges the JSON parsing library's pluggable allocation interface to the talloc hierarchical memory manager so that all JSON-allocated memory is tracked under a talloc context.

scope.in-scope: Construction of an allocator object that routes JSON library allocation calls through talloc.
scope.boundary: The allocator object produced by this component is passed directly to the JSON library. The library uses it whenever it needs to allocate, reallocate, or free memory during document parsing and construction.

Allocations are made as talloc children of the context provided at allocator creation. Frees are performed directly on the pointer without needing the parent context, which is the expected behavior of talloc.

behavior.allocator-creation:
An allocator object is created by providing a talloc context. The result is returned by value (not heap-allocated). The allocator carries three operations:
When the JSON library requests memory of a given size, it is allocated as a talloc child of the context provided at allocator creation.
When the JSON library requests a resize, the pointer is reallocated to the new size. The old size argument provided by the JSON library is ignored — talloc tracks allocation sizes internally.
When the JSON library releases memory, the pointer is freed via talloc. The context argument provided by the JSON library is ignored — talloc does not require the parent context to free a child.
